name: Ensure there are no weak password policies
body:
### Security intent

This control validates that **there are no weak password policies** across Okta.

### Access condition reviewed

The assessment focuses on there that are not no weak password policies. It reviews the identity, credential, role, or policy state that creates the access condition described by the control.

### Practitioner note

Identity findings deserve careful cleanup rather than blind deletion. Confirm ownership and business need, remove unnecessary standing access, and prefer federation, groups, roles, MFA, and short-lived credentials over direct long-lived grants.
description: There that are not no weak password policies
remediationDescription:
### Okta

1. Open the Okta Admin Console and go to Security > Authenticators.
2. Edit the Password authenticator.
3. Increase minimum password length and complexity to match the organization password standard.
4. Disable weak or legacy password options such as overly short passwords, password reuse, or unlimited failed attempts.
5. Configure lockout and recovery behavior so brute-force attempts are slowed and recovery remains controlled.
6. Apply the policy to the intended groups and test with a standard user before broad rollout.

Reference: https://help.okta.com/oie/en-us/content/topics/identity-engine/authenticators/configure-password.htm
